name: Rhai
description: An embedded scripting language and evaluation engine for Rust with JavaScript+Rust-like syntax

language-family.comment: Described as 'JavaScript+Rust' syntax with dynamic typing

paradigm.comment: Supports imperative, functional (via closures/function pointers), and some OOP patterns

first-class-functions.comment: Dynamic dispatch via function pointers with currying

closures.comment: Closures that can capture shared variables

pattern-matching.comment: Switch statements with pattern matching, ranges, conditions, and default cases

async-await.comment: No native async in scripts. Engine can be Send+Sync via 'sync' feature for use in async contexts

iterators-generators.comment: Built-in iterator support with for loops and iteration methods

standard-library.comment: Built-in functions for strings, arrays, maps, math, etc. Disableable via feature flags

compilation-model.comment: Can eval directly or precompile to AST for repeated execution

hot-reloading.comment: Scripts can be recompiled and re-evaluated at runtime

repl-support.comment: REPL available via optional rustyline dependency

debugging-support.comment: Debugging interface available via feature flag

source-maps.comment: Position tracking during parsing, can be disabled for performance

execution-speed.comment: AST-walking interpreter; claims '1M iterations in 0.14s' on single-core Linux VM

memory-overhead.comment: Small footprint, designed for embedded use

garbage-collection.comment: Uses Rust's ownership semantics with clone-on-write and reference counting for shared values

pure-rust.comment: No C/C++ dependencies. Dependencies: smallvec, thin-vec, num-traits, once_cell, ahash, bitflags, smartstring

api-safety.comment: Don't Panic guarantee - any panic is considered a bug

idiomatic-api.comment: Good derive macros, builder patterns, Result types. Some verbosity for complex registrations

async-compatible.comment: Scripts execute synchronously. 'sync' feature makes Engine Send+Sync for use in async contexts

function-registration.comment: Derive macros, register_fn, and low-level register_raw_fn

type-mapping.comment: Automatic conversion for common types, serde for complex types

no-std-compatible.comment: no_std builds supported with additional dependencies

wasm-compatible.comment: WASM builds supported, pulls in web-time and other WASM-specific dependencies

blank-slate-mode.comment: Can disable all built-ins via feature flags: no_index, no_object, no_function, no_module, etc.

memory-sandboxing.comment: Scripts cannot access arbitrary host memory

cpu-limits.comment: Operation count limits, execution time limits via progress callback

syscall-restrictions.comment: No built-in file/network I/O - all capabilities must be explicitly registered

capability-security.comment: Feature flags and selective registration control what scripts can access

default-deny.comment: Scripts can only access explicitly registered functions and types

share-data.comment: Primarily clone-based, but references possible via read_lock/write_lock for first argument

method-syntax.comment: Tight integration with native Rust functions and types, including getters/setters, methods and indexers

callbacks-events.comment: Scripts can pass function pointers for callbacks

crates-io-downloads.comment: Estimated based on ~217k monthly downloads over project lifetime

package-manager.comment: No official package ecosystem, but extension crates exist (rhai-sci, rhai-rand, rhai-http)

editor-support.comment: VS Code extension available for syntax highlighting

example-projects.comment: Used in 367 crates including game engines and configuration systems

maintenance-status.comment: Regular releases, active issue resolution, version 1.24.0 released Jan 2026

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive book with tutorials, API reference, migration guides, and extensive examples

maturity.comment: First released 2016, now at v1.24.0 with stable API
